title: ACS Clinical Congress
audience: Academic surgeons, residents, fellows, and surgical educators
submissionType: Scientific papers, posters, and video sessions
description: A flagship surgical meeting with broad clinical, educational, and research programming.
collaborationFocus: Multi-institution outcomes research and surgical education collaboration.

title: Academic Surgical Congress
organizer: Association for Academic Surgery and Society of University Surgeons
audience: Surgeon-scientists, trainees, research faculty, and academic leaders
submissionType: Original research, quickshot presentations, and posters
description: A research-driven forum centered on innovation, mentorship, and academic career development.
collaborationFocus: Mentorship, trainee scholarship, and cross-institution research networks.

title: AAST Annual Meeting
organizer: American Association for the Surgery of Trauma
audience: Trauma surgeons, acute care surgeons, fellows, and emergency surgery researchers
submissionType: Podium abstracts, posters, and multicenter study updates
description: An acute care and trauma surgery meeting focused on evidence, systems, and team-based care.
collaborationFocus: Trauma registries, multicenter trials, and acute care surgery research.

title: SSAT Annual Meeting
organizer: Society for Surgery of the Alimentary Tract
audience: GI surgeons, translational scientists, and digestive disease specialists
submissionType: Oral presentations, posters, and video case sessions
description: A digestive surgery meeting that blends translational science, clinical techniques, and academic networking.
collaborationFocus: Translational GI research and society-driven research working groups.

title: STS Annual Meeting
audience: Cardiothoracic surgeons, trainees, perfusion teams, and outcomes researchers
submissionType: Scientific abstracts, case videos, and late-breaking trials
description: A modern cardiothoracic meeting with a strong emphasis on outcomes, technology, and education.
collaborationFocus: Registry science, simulation, and collaborative quality improvement.
